scheme_name: Self-Employment Scheme: Transport/ Logistics/ Travels/ Food Court Wyaaj Sahaay
page_title: Self-Employment Scheme: Transport/ Logistics/ Travels/ Food Court Wyaaj Sahaay

details: "Transport/ Logistics/ Travels/ Food Court Wyaaj Sahaay" is a component under the "Self-Employment Scheme" introduced by the Social Justice and Empowerment Department, Gujarat. Under this scheme, eligible beneficiaries can avail of a 5% interest subsidy on a bank loan of ₹6.00 lakh for purchasing vehicles and setting up the required infrastructure for businesses like transport, logistics, travelers, food courts, etc.
benefits: A 5% subsidy on the interest for a bank loan of ₹6,00,000/-.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Gujarat.
The applicant must belong to the unreserved category.
The applicant's age should be between 18 years and 50 years.
The applicant must have a valid driving license.
The vehicle purchased under the scheme must be mortgaged (hypothecated) to the corporation.
The applicant must be willing to repay the loan in equal monthly installments over 5 years.
The annual income limit of the family should be ₹6,00,000/- or less.
The interest rate on the loan will be 5 percent simple interest per annum.
The simple interest will be calculated based on the amount of the loan given per year.
Only 1 person from a family can avail of the scheme benefits.
A maximum of 5 beneficiaries per village will be selected.

online: and navigate to the "Gujarat Unreserved Education and Economical Development Corporation" section under the Corporation menu.
Click on "Transport/ Logistics/ Travels/ Food Court Wyaaj Sahaay" under "Self-Employment Scheme" and then Apply Now.
Click on "New User? Please Register Here".
Enter the required details and click Register.
Enter your User ID, Password, and Captcha Code, then click "Login".
Read the scheme details carefully and click OK.
If your permanent address is correct, confirm it. Otherwise, enter your correct address.
Click Save & Next to proceed. Upload the relevant documents.
If you need to make changes, click "No, Cancel". If all details are correct, click "Yes, Submit".
Click Save Application. Note down your Application Number for future reference. You can also take a printout by clicking Print Application.

After submitting the online application, the applicant must take a printout of the application, sign it, and submit it along with all uploaded documents (certificates, mark sheets, etc.) to the concerned Deputy Director (Non-Reserved Category) or the District Social Welfare Officer through courier, post, or by visiting in person.

documents_required: Application form in the prescribed format for self-employment

faqs.question: What is the "Transport/ Logistics/ Travels/ Food Court Wyaaj Sahaay" scheme?
faqs.answer: "Transport/ Logistics/ Travels/ Food Court Wyaaj Sahaay" is a component under the "Self-Employment Scheme" that provides 5% interest subsidy on a bank loan of ₹6,00,000/- for purchasing vehicles and setting up the required infrastructure.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Any Indian citizen who is engaged in transport, logistics, travel, or food court businesses and has taken a loan of ₹6,00,000/- for business purposes is eligible to apply.

faqs.question: How are beneficiaries selected under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Beneficiaries are selected through a draw system, ensuring a fair and transparent selection process.

faqs.question: How will an applicant know if they have been selected?
faqs.answer: Selected applicants will receive a notification via email or SMS after the draw process.

faqs.question: What happens if a selected applicant does not meet the eligibility criteria?
faqs.answer: If a selected applicant fails to meet the prescribed eligibility criteria, their application will be rejected.

faqs.question: Can more than one person from the same family apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: No, only one person per family can avail of the benefits under this scheme.

faqs.question: What is the maximum number of beneficiaries allowed from a single village?
faqs.answer: A maximum of five beneficiaries per village can be selected under this scheme.

faqs.question: Are women given priority in the selection process?
faqs.answer: Yes, at least 10% of selected applicants will be women, ensuring gender inclusivity.

faqs.question: What is the deadline for submitting documents after selection?
faqs.answer: Selected applicants must upload the required documents online within 7 days and submit a printed copy to the District Manager’s office within 30 days.

faqs.question: Can an applicant apply for this scheme without taking a bank loan?
faqs.answer: No, only those who have taken a ₹6.00 lakh bank loan for acquiring vehicles along with the necessary structure are eligible for the scheme.
